title: NOC 32110 – Denturists | GTR Immigration
description: Denturists under NOC 32110 are specialized dental health professionals who design, construct, fit, and repair dentures across Canada. With increasing demand for oral healthcare services—particularly among aging populations—Denturists continue to play an essential role in Canada’s dental care system. As a TEER 2 occupation, Denturists qualify under multiple economic immigration pathways, making this profession a structured option for Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER2 – College diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group32 – Technical occupations in healthSub-Major Group321 – Technical occupations in health (except practitioners of natural healing)Minor Group3211 – Technical occupations in dental health careNOC Code32110

duties: Denturists perform some or all of the following duties:
Measure patients’ jaws to determine denture size and shape
Take impressions of teeth, gums, and jaws
Fit, insert, and modify new dentures
employment_requirements: Completion of a two- or three-year college program in denturism or denturology
Internship in a registered denture clinic may be required
Licensing by a provincial or territorial regulatory body is mandatory
College of Denturists of British Columbia
Internationally educated Denturists must complete credential assessment and provincial licensing prior to practice.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $27,500 to $112,400, depending on experience, province, and whether operating a private practice.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 32110 remains stable, with modest growth driven by:
Increasing demand for removable dental prosthetics
Denturists with strong technical skills and provincial licensure may benefit from self-employment opportunities.
labour_market_demand: This positive employment balance reflects moderate but stable demand. Provinces with stronger activity include Ontario and Quebec, where aging populations and established regulatory frameworks support the profession.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 32110?
Growing demand due to aging population
Access to universal healthcare and settlement benefits
Dental prosthetic services remain essential across both public and private healthcare settings.
pathways: If you are a qualified Denturist, NOC 32110 offers a structured immigration pathway to Canada.
Express Entry and PNP eligibility assessment
Contact GTR Immigration today to begin your pathway toward permanent residence in Canada under NOC 32110.
